message.content:
=== THE LAW (violating it burns the artifact regardless of score) ===
A source may enter the corpus ONLY bound to real bytes through provenance.py: manifestation_id +
content_hash, and for task 72's JOURNAL-ONLY policy it must be journal-attributable (a working paper is a
DISCOVERY LEAD, not journal evidence — peer review changes numbers; Acemoglu's robot effect was 0.37pp in
the NBER WP and 0.2pp in the JPE). Every fetch goes through acquisition.Acquirer, which emits observations,
never statuses. A 429 is THROTTLED -> BACKEND_FAILED, NEVER "no evidence exists".

=== THE TWO GUARDRAILS — WE HAVE HURT OURSELVES ON BOTH BEFORE ===
1. MAX THE SEARCH, NOT THE COUNT. Sol: the objective is NOT "100 papers" or "202 numbers" — it is ENOUGH
   INDEPENDENT, INTERPRETABLE EVIDENCE to close the question's cells and create high-value CONTRASTS.
   bodhi scores 0.5441 with ONE venue mention. So: cast the widest possible net, but ADMIT ONLY genuinely
   on-topic journal articles. A keyword net once dragged in ResNet, SMOTE, skin-cancer classification and
   the European Heart Journal ('work' is a substring of 'network'). Semantic on-topic gate, WORD BOUNDARIES.
2. GENERALITY. Run the CONTRACT-DRIVEN pipeline (research_contract -> source_router), NOT a hardcoded
   task-72 regex. We are running it ON task 72; we are not baking task 72 into the code.

=== DO NOT COLLIDE ===
Two other wheels are live (composer wiring, generality). Write ONLY to outputs/acquisition_campaign/.
Do NOT touch cellcog_composer.py, outputs/journal_corpus_content.json, or outputs/evidence_cards_bound.json.
acquisition.py's ledger is append-only union — safe to share.


=== WHERE WE ARE ===
10 admissible journal works (the honest post-quarantine corpus). cellcog #1 has ~98 sources; bodhi #2 ~33.
This is a FLOOR, not SOTA. The seed works, with DOIs to expand from:
  Acemoglu 2019 JEP | Goos 2014 AER | Damioli 2021 Eurasian Bus Rev | Schwabe 2020 PLOS ONE |
  Tolan 2021 J.AI Research | Cao 2021 Technovation | Braganza 2021 J.Bus.Research | Raj 2019 J.Org.Design |
  Ayling 2022 AI&Ethics | Chalmers 2021 Entrep.Theory&Practice

EMPTY COVERAGE CELLS (the outline promises sections we cannot support): healthcare, manufacturing, retail,
finance, education, agriculture, transport. And our corpus ENDS IN 2023 — the entire generative-AI turn
(2023-2025) is missing, and the judge rewards current evidence.

TOOLS ON DISK (read them, use them, do not rebuild): source_router.py (+ config/source_routes.yaml),
recency.py, gap_search.py, insight_value.py, weighting.py, journal_corpus_build.py (has citation-graph
expansion that once took us 22->70 papers), deep_fetch.py, wp_fetch.py, acquisition.py, provenance.py,
research_contract.py.


MODALITY 3 — THE 2023-2025 FRONTIER. Our corpus ends in 2023; the generative-AI turn is missing and the
judge rewards current evidence. Use recency.py's frontier lane: search publication/online dates in
overlapping bands (since 2023, last 24 months, last 12 months, online-ahead-of-print) via Crossref's DATE
filters + OpenAlex, SORTED BY DATE not citations (recent papers have no citations yet — that is the whole
point). Target: generative AI / LLMs / ChatGPT and work, employment, wages, tasks, productivity, occupations,
skill demand. Keep only journal articles (or clearly-labelled journal versions), genuinely on-topic. Return
doi/title/venue/year/why_relevant/cell_served, deduped, counts by year. Do NOT fetch full text yet. Report
how much 2023-2025 journal literature is actually reachable.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""RECENCY — the frontier the citation graph cannot reach, searched BY DATE.  (Sol plan 4, item 3.)

THE GAP THIS CLOSES
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Our retrieval is BACKWARD: it looks a work up by DOI and walks its references and citers. Sol:

    "'Just search by date' is correct because BACKWARD CITATION EXPANSION SYSTEMATICALLY MISSES RECENT
     WORK: recent papers have not accumulated references or citations."

Our corpus ends in 2023. The generative-AI turn is 2023-2025 and the citation graph literally cannot
reach it — the edges do not exist yet. So this module does the one thing the graph can't: it issues
DIRECT, DATE-WINDOWED queries and SORTS THEM BY DATE, never by citation count.

TWO INDEPENDENT LANES
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  FOUNDATION — seminal theories, landmark methods, long-run evidence. Found by citation weight /
               relevance, NO date window, and NO recency penalty for age. A 1979 theorem is not stale.
  FRONTIER   — explicit publication-date windows, searched directly and SORTED BY the DATE FIELD,
               never by citation count. For task 72 the frontier begins at the generative-AI boundary
               and sweeps OVERLAPPING bands: since 2023 | last 24 months | last 12 months |
               accepted/online-ahead-of-print | newly-indexed-since-last-run.

WHERE THE INSIGHT LIVES (and where it does NOT)
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Every window, every boundary date, every per-database date field, and the whole claim->recency policy
live in config/authority/recency_frontier_profile.yaml — DATA. This file contains NO topic gate, NO
regex over a question, and NO domain date. It cannot tell you that AI=2023 or that migraine needs a
trial-completion window; it can only ask the profile. A new domain is a new registry row, never an
edit here (LAW: generality is a DATA edit).

RECENCY IS CLAIM-SPECIFIC
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
The claim's `claim_kind` (carried on the contract, not sniffed here) selects a policy row:
  * a foundational theory  -> foundation lane only, no age penalty;
  * a current adoption rate -> frontier, keyed on publication/online/indexed dates;
  * a clinical conclusion  -> frontier keyed on publication + trial-completion + registry + results
                              dates, PLUS a corrections/retractions pull;
  * a current statute      -> frontier keyed on the EFFECTIVE text and SUBSEQUENT TREATMENT — the
                              newest commentary is explicitly NOT a substitute for the in-force law;
  * a thin-evidence claim  -> a date-windowed search that returns nothing licenses "the recent
                              literature does not settle this", and NEVER "the field proves no effect".

DATE FIELDS ARE NOT INTERCHANGEABLE
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
publication vs online vs accepted vs posted vs registration vs indexed vs updated vs trial-completion
are SEPARATE fields (Crossref exposes them as separate filters). The registry maps each database to
ONLY the date-types it truly exposes; a band that keys on a field a database lacks is emitted as
UNSUPPORTED with a reason — it is never silently re-pointed at a different date.

THE PROBE CANNOT CONCLUDE
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Every network touch goes through acquisition.Acquirer, which emits OBSERVATIONS, never statuses. A
frontier band that returns nothing becomes the sentence "no recent eligible evidence located" ONLY
when event_ledger.derive_coverage_status reduces the cell to SEARCHED_NONE (the adapters answered and
were empty). A THROTTLED / BLOCKED band is SEARCH_FAILED and licenses NO absence — a fact about our
request rate must never be written to disk as a fact about the recent literature.

    python3 scripts/recency.py            # build + print the bands for task 72 and a clinical question
    python3 scripts/recency.py --probe    # additionally run the frontier bands live (uses Acquirer)

384	    fields = (db.get('date_fields') or {}).get(date_type)
385	    if not fields:
386	        exposes = ', '.join(sorted((db.get('date_fields') or {}))) or '(none)'
387	        return DatabaseQuery(
388	            db_name, band.key, date_type, supported=False,
389	            note=f'{db_name} does not expose a {date_type!r} date field (it exposes: {exposes}); '
390	                 f'NOT substituting another date — the fields are not interchangeable')
391	    if band.since is None:
392	        return DatabaseQuery(db_name, band.key, date_type, supported=False,
393	                             note=band.note or 'no window')
394	
395	    style = db['style']
396	    q = need.query_string()

401	        params = {'query': q, fields['from_year']: yr, 'sort': fields['sort'],

403	        url = f"{db['base']}?{urllib.parse.urlencode(params)}"
404	        return DatabaseQuery(db_name, band.key, date_type, True, url,
405	                             fields['from_year'], fields['sort'],
406	                             note='bulk endpoint only — the plain /search endpoint sorts by relevance/'
407	                                  'citation and must not be used for a frontier band')
408	
409	    driver = _DRIVERS.get(style)
410	    if driver is None:
411	        return DatabaseQuery(db_name, band.key, date_type, False, note=f'no driver for style {style!r}')
412	    if style == 'courtlistener_filed':
413	        url, dfield, sort = driver(db, fields, q, since, until, content_frag, need.target_id)
414	    else:

418	    note = ''

421	        return DatabaseQuery(db_name, band.key, date_type, False,
422	                             note=f'REFUSED: {db_name} sort {sort!r} is the citation sort — the '
423	                                  f'frontier lane sorts by date, never by citation count')
424	    if sort is None:

449	                db = profile['databases'].get(db_name)

484	def build_foundation_queries(need: RecencyNeed, profile: dict) -> list[FoundationQuery]:
485	    """Landmark discovery. NO date filter, sorted by citation weight / relevance. Age is NOT penalized.
486	
487	    Only runs when the claim's policy includes the foundation lane. Note this is the ONE place a
488	    citation sort is CORRECT — a landmark IS the highly-cited work — which is exactly why the frontier
489	    lane, whose whole job is to find work that has not yet BEEN cited, must never use it.
490	    """
491	    pol = _policy_for(need, profile)
492	    if 'foundation' not in (pol.get('lanes') or []):
493	        return []
494	    q = need.query_string()
495	    out: list[FoundationQuery] = []
496	    for db_name in (pol.get('databases') or []):

505	        elif style == 'openalex_filter':

507	            url = f"{db['base']}?" + urllib.parse.urlencode(
508	                {'filter': f'default.search:{q}', 'sort': sort, 'per-page': db.get('rows', 40),
509	                 **({'mailto': MAILTO} if db.get('mailto') else {})})
510	        elif style == 'semanticscholar_bulk':
511	            sort = cit or 'relevance'
512	            url = f"{db['base']}?" + urllib.parse.urlencode(
513	                {'query': q, 'sort': sort, 'fields': 'title,year,citationCount,externalIds'})

516	            continue

518	                                   note='no date window; age carries no penalty (foundation lane)'))

526	def plan(need: RecencyNeed, profile: dict | None = None) -> RecencyPlan:
527	    profile = profile if profile is not None else load_profile()
528	    pol = _policy_for(need, profile)
529	    bands = resolve_bands(need, profile)
530	    on_empty = (pol.get('on_empty') or
531	                'no recent eligible evidence located in {window}; a scoped absence, not a disproof')
532	    return RecencyPlan(
533	        need=need, policy=pol, lanes=list(pol.get('lanes') or []),
534	        foundation=build_foundation_queries(need, profile),
535	        frontier_bands=bands,
536	        frontier_queries=build_frontier_queries(need, profile, bands),
537	        content_filters=list(pol.get('content_filters') or []),
538	        on_empty_template=on_empty)

541	# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
542	# THE PROBE — uses acquisition.Acquirer and CANNOT conclude
543	# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
544	
545	class FrontierProbe:
546	    """Runs the frontier bands through THE ONE DOOR (acquisition.Acquirer). It records observations and
547	    defers every conclusion to the ledger's reducer — it never reads a None as 'no recent evidence'."""
548	
549	    def __init__(self, acq: Acquirer, profile: dict):
550	        self.acq = acq
551	        self.profile = profile
552	
553	    def run(self, plan_: RecencyPlan, *, max_queries: int | None = None) -> dict:
554	        """Execute the supported frontier queries. Returns a per-band summary. NOTHING here concludes
555	        absence: a band that returns zero results emits NO candidate, so the reducer can later see an
556	        adequately-routed, empty cell (SEARCHED_NONE) — while a THROTTLED band stays SEARCH_FAILED."""
557	        unit = plan_.need.unit or f'recency:{plan_.need.claim_kind}'
558	        supported = [q for q in plan_.frontier_queries if q.supported]
559	        if max_queries:
560	            supported = supported[:max_queries]
561	        # ROUTE_PLANNED names the adapters we are ABOUT to try, so route completion is meaningful.
562	        adapters = [f'{q.database}:{q.band}:{q.date_type}' for q in supported]
563	        self.acq.plan_route(unit, adapters, lane='frontier', claim_kind=plan_.need.claim_kind)
564	        results = []
565	        for q in supported:
566	            adapter = f'{q.database}:{q.band}:{q.date_type}'
567	            resp, body = self.acq.get_json(unit, adapter, q.url)
568	            n = _result_count(self.profile['databases'].get(q.database, {}), body) if resp.ok else None
569	            if resp.ok and n:
570	                # a real hit becomes a CANDIDATE — evidence identification, still not a conclusion
571	                self.acq.candidate(unit, adapter, q.url, n_results=n, date_type=q.date_type,
572	                                   band=q.band, sort=q.sort)
573	            results.append({'adapter': adapter, 'ok': resp.ok, 'outcome': resp.outcome,
574	                            'http_status': resp.http_status, 'n_results': n})
575	        return {'unit': unit, 'n_queries': len(supported), 'results': results}
576	
577	    def absence_is_licensed(self, need: RecencyNeed, on_empty_template: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:
578	        """Ask THE REDUCER whether this cell licenses a scoped-absence sentence. Only SEARCHED_NONE
579	        does. THROTTLED/BLOCKED reduce to SEARCH_FAILED and license nothing — the acquisition invariant
580	        carried all the way to the sentence."""
581	        unit = need.unit or f'recency:{need.claim_kind}'
582	        status, info = derive_coverage_status(self.acq.ledger, unit)
583	        if status == SEARCHED_NONE:
584	            return True, on_empty_template
585	        return False, (f'no absence sentence: coverage is {status} — {info.get("reason", "")}. '
586	                       f'A fact about our search is not a fact about the recent literature.')

589	def _result_count(db: dict, body: Any) -> int:
590	    """Read the hit count by walking the database's DATA-declared `count_path` through the JSON body.
591	
592	    There is NO per-source branch here: a new database contributes its own count_path in the profile
593	    and the probe reads it. This is only ever used to decide whether to emit a CANDIDATE; it is never
594	    the basis of an absence conclusion — that is the reducer's job, over the ledger."""
595	    path = (db or {}).get('count_path')
596	    if not path or not isinstance(body, (dict, list)):
597	        return 0
598	    cur: Any = body
599	    for key in path:
600	        if isinstance(cur, dict):
601	            cur = cur.get(key)
602	        else:
603	            return 0
604	    try:
605	        return int(cur)
606	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
607	        return 0

1	# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
2	# RECENCY / FRONTIER PROFILE  —  Sol plan 4, item 3.  VERSIONED DATA (LAW VI).
3	#
4	# Consumed by scripts/recency.py. NOTHING in this file is a code path; EVERYTHING here is a registry
5	# row. A new domain (a new drug, a new statute, a new frontier boundary, a new database) is added by
6	# EDITING THIS FILE, never by editing recency.py. The tell we keep failing — a hardcoded topic gate,
7	# a hand-written regex, a domain insight baked into code — is structurally impossible if the insight
8	# lives only here.
9	#
10	# WHY THIS EXISTS (Sol, verbatim): "'Just search by date' is correct because BACKWARD CITATION
11	# EXPANSION SYSTEMATICALLY MISSES RECENT WORK: recent papers have not accumulated references or
12	# citations." Our corpus ends in 2023; the generative-AI turn is 2023-2025 and the citation graph
13	# cannot reach it, because the edges do not exist yet. The frontier must be searched DIRECTLY, BY DATE.
14	# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
15	
16	schema_version: recency-frontier-1
17	
18	# Fallback window for a claim date-type that none of the named `bands` below cover (e.g. a clinical
19	# trial-completion window, a legal effective-date window). Used only when no band keys on that
20	# date-type: [boundary.since, as_of] when a boundary is named, else [as_of - N months, as_of].
21	default_frontier_months: 24
22	
23	
24	# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
25	# FRONTIER BOUNDARIES
26	# A named epoch a claim's frontier begins at. A RecencyNeed names ONE of these BY KEY (the key comes
27	# from the research contract, not from a regex over the question). Add a domain => add a row here.
28	# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
29	frontier_boundaries:
30	  generative_ai_turn:
31	    since: "2023-01-01"
32	    label: "the generative-AI turn"
33	    rationale: >-
34	      Work published after this date has not yet accumulated the references or citations a
35	      citation-graph crawl follows, so backward expansion cannot see it. Search the window directly.
36	  covid_era:
37	    since: "2020-01-01"
38	    label: "the COVID-19 era"
39	    rationale: "A discontinuity after which prior baselines may not hold; recent evidence is required."
40	  post_dobbs:
41	    since: "2022-06-24"

187	class Response:
188	    """What came back. `outcome` is an OBSERVATION about the transport, never about the literature.
189	
190	    THERE IS NO `.absent` AND THERE NEVER WILL BE. The whole disease was one line — `if not d: return
191	    []` — in which four transport outcomes became a claim about the world. A caller may branch on
192	    `.ok`; if it wants to know whether a copy EXISTS it must ask a reducer, over the ledger, which
193	    knows the difference between "they answered, and it is not there" and "we were throttled".
194	    """
195	    outcome: str
196	    http_status: int | None
197	    raw: bytes
198	    content_type: str
199	    url: str
200	    adapter: str
201	    request_id: str

247	class Acquirer:
248	    """Emits observations. Cannot emit a status: no method here takes one.
249	
250	    Every method is a thin, boring wrapper over `Ledger.emit`, and that is the point — the interesting
251	    part of this class is what it DOES NOT EXPOSE.
252	    """
253	
254	    def __init__(self, actor: str, ledger: Ledger | None = None, blobs: BlobStore | None = None):
255	        self.actor = actor
256	        self.ledger = ledger if ledger is not None else open_ledger()
257	        self.blobs = blobs if blobs is not None else BlobStore()
258	
259	    # -- 1. ROUTE_PLANNED -------------------------------------------------------------------------
260	    def plan_route(self, unit: str, adapters: Iterable[str], **obs) -> None:
261	        """`route_complete` will mean EVERY ONE OF THESE HAS A TERMINAL OUTCOME RECORD.
262	
263	        It must never mean "an adapter was mapped", so this is called BEFORE the loop, with the
264	        adapters we are ABOUT to try — not after, with the ones that happened to answer.
265	        """
266	        self.ledger.emit(unit, EventKind.ROUTE_PLANNED, self.actor,
267	                         adapters=list(adapters), **obs)
268	
269	    # -- 2 & 3. THE EXCEPTION BOUNDARY ------------------------------------------------------------
270	    def get(self, unit: str, adapter: str, url: str, *, tries: int = 4, timeout: int = 30,
271	            **obs) -> Response:
272	        """One logical request. BACKEND_ATTEMPTED before each attempt; EXACTLY ONE of
273	        RESPONSE_RECEIVED | THROTTLED | BLOCKED at the exception boundary of each attempt.
274	
275	        THE RETRIES SHARE A `request_id`. That is load-bearing: a 429 that a backoff RESOLVED and a
276	        429 that we GAVE UP ON are different facts, and the reducer must be able to tell them apart
277	        without either of them being deleted from the log. Both are here, forever; the reducer reads
278	        the request's FINAL terminal event.
279	
280	          exhausted 429s -> the last terminal event is THROTTLED -> BACKEND_FAILED. FOREVER.
281	          429 then 200   -> the last terminal event is RESPONSE_RECEIVED -> RESPONDED. The 429 is
282	                            still on disk, because the backoff working is not the same as it never
283	                            having been throttled.
284	        """
285	        rid = f'{adapter}#{next(_RID)}'
286	        last: Response | None = None
287	        for attempt in range(1, max(1, tries) + 1):
288	            self._space(url)
289	            self.ledger.emit(unit, EventKind.BACKEND_ATTEMPTED, self.actor,
290	                             adapter=adapter, url=url, request_id=rid, attempt=attempt, **obs)
291	            try:
292	                req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers=UA)
293	                # NOTE: `urllib.request.urlopen` is resolved AT CALL TIME, on purpose. The adversary
294	                # harness monkeypatches it to force a 429 storm, and a `from ... import urlopen` would
295	                # bind past the patch and test nothing.
296	                with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=timeout) as r:
297	                    raw = r.read()
298	                    ctype = r.headers.get('Content-Type', '') or ''
299	                    code = int(getattr(r, 'status', 200) or 200)
300	                self.ledger.emit(unit, EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED, self.actor,
301	                                 adapter=adapter, url=url, request_id=rid, attempt=attempt,
302	                                 http_status=code, content_type=ctype, n_bytes=len(raw))
303	                return Response(RESPONDED, code, raw, ctype, url, adapter, rid)
304	
305	            except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
306	                code = int(getattr(e, 'code', 0) or 0)
307	
308	                if code in THROTTLE_CODES:
309	                    # ** A 429 PERSISTS AS THROTTLED. IT CANNOT BECOME CITATION_ONLY. **
310	                    self.ledger.emit(unit, EventKind.THROTTLED, self.actor,
311	                                     adapter=adapter, url=url, request_id=rid, attempt=attempt,
312	                                     http_status=code, retry_after=self._retry_after(e))
313	                    last = Response(THROTTLED, code, b'', '', url, adapter, rid, f'HTTP {code}')
314	                    if attempt < tries:
315	                        self._backoff(attempt)
316	                        continue
317	                    return last
318	
319	                if code in BLOCK_CODES:
320	                    # A fact about ENTITLEMENT. Retrying a paywall is not politeness, it is noise.
321	                    self.ledger.emit(unit, EventKind.BLOCKED, self.actor,
322	                                     adapter=adapter, url=url, request_id=rid, attempt=attempt,
323	                                     http_status=code)
324	                    return Response(BLOCKED, code, b'', '', url, adapter, rid, f'HTTP {code}')
325	
326	                if code == 404:
327	                    # THE BACKEND ANSWERED. "My index does not have this" is a RESPONSE, and it is a
328	                    # fact about THEIR INDEX — not about whether the paper is free somewhere else.
329	                    # (Semantic Scholar 404s the QJE DOI for Autor/Levy/Murnane. NBER WP 8337 exists.)
330	                    self.ledger.emit(unit, EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED, self.actor,
331	                                     adapter=adapter, url=url, request_id=rid, attempt=attempt,
332	                                     http_status=404, n_bytes=0)
333	                    return Response(NOT_INDEXED, 404, b'', '', url, adapter, rid)

  current evidence. Backward citation expansion CANNOT reach recent work (the edges don't exist yet), so
  we search DIRECTLY BY DATE using recency.py's FRONTIER LANE:
    * overlapping date bands (since the 2023 gen-AI boundary | last 24m | last 12m | online-ahead-of-print
      | newly-indexed) PLUS explicit per-calendar-year windows (2023 | 2024 | 2025) so a date-sorted-desc
      sweep — which otherwise bunches at 2026 — samples every year;
    * SORTED BY DATE, never by citation count (recency.py REFUSES the citation sort — that is the point);
    * via Crossref DATE filters + OpenAlex, both named in the task.
  Every network touch goes through acquisition.Acquirer (THE ONE DOOR) — it emits observations, never
  statuses. A 429 is THROTTLED, not "no evidence". We DO NOT fetch full text (metadata + inline abstract only).

WHAT IT IS NOT
  Not a hardcoded task-72 gate baked into the pipeline. The frontier machinery (recency.py, its profile) is
  untouched and general. The on-topic term lists + coverage cells below are DATA for THIS retrieval report,
  written only to outputs/acquisition_campaign/. The admission rule is the guardrail, made concrete:
  ADMIT ONLY genuinely on-topic JOURNAL articles — (AI term) AND (a CORE labor term), WORD BOUNDARIES,
  working papers/preprints excluded (a working paper is a DISCOVERY LEAD, not journal evidence).
"""
from __future__ import annotations
import sys, os, re, json, html, datetime as dt
from pathlib import Path
from collections import defaultdict, Counter

HERE = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
FLYWHEEL = HERE.parent.parent                      # /home/polaris/wt/flywheel
sys.path.insert(0, str(FLYWHEEL / 'scripts'))

import recency as R                                # the FRONTIER LANE
from acquisition import Acquirer, open_ledger      # THE ONE DOOR

AS_OF = dt.date(2026, 7, 14)
ROWS = 100                                         # deeper sample per query (rows is DATA; bumped in memory)

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# ON-TOPIC VOCABULARY  (DATA for this report — word-boundary regexes; NOT baked into the shared pipeline)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Generative-AI-specific terms (the missing 2023-2025 turn). A match here flags genai_specific=True.
AI_GENERATIVE = [
    r"generative a\.?\s?i\b", r"gen[- ]?ai\b", r"generative artificial intelligence",
    r"large language models?", r"\bllms?\b", r"chatgpt", r"gpt-?[0-9]", r"\bgpt\b",
    r"foundation models?", r"generative pre-?trained", r"diffusion models?", r"stable diffusion",
    r"dall-?e\b", r"\bcopilot\b", r"midjourney", r"conversational a\.?\s?i\b", r"text-to-image",
]
# Broader AI terms (a 2023+ journal article may frame the gen-AI turn under plain "AI"/"automation").
AI_BROAD = [
    r"artificial intelligence", r"machine learning", r"deep learning", r"neural networks?",
    r"\bautomation\b", r"automat(?:e|ed|ing|ion)", r"robot(?:s|ic|ics)?\b", r"algorithmic management",
]
# CORE labor/work anchors (STRONG — an unambiguous employment signal). Admission REQUIRES one of these.
LABOR_CORE = [
    r"\bemployment\b", r"\bunemployment\b", r"labou?r market", r"labou?r demand", r"labou?r force",
    r"labou?r supply", r"\bwages?\b", r"\bearnings\b", r"\bsalar(?:y|ies)\b",
    r"\bjobs?\b", r"job displacement", r"job loss(?:es)?", r"job creation", r"job destruction",
    r"\boccupations?\b", r"\boccupational\b", r"\bworkforce\b", r"\bworkers?\b", r"\bworkplace\b",
    r"\bhiring\b", r"recruit(?:ment|ing|er)", r"\breskilling\b", r"\bupskilling\b", r"human capital",
    r"\bemployees?\b", r"skills? demand", r"demand for skills", r"gig (?:economy|work|worker)",
    r"self-employ", r"freelanc(?:e|er|ing)", r"labou?r productivity", r"worker productivity",
    r"task displacement", r"task automation", r"future of work", r"routine tasks?", r"job tasks?",
    r"work tasks?", r"skill premium", r"wage inequality", r"labou?r substitut",
]
# Working-paper / preprint venues — EXCLUDED from journal evidence (they are discovery leads only).
NONJOURNAL_VENUE = re.compile(
    r"working paper|discussion paper|\bnber\b|\bssrn\b|\biza\b|\bcepr\b|\bcesifo\b|mimeo|preprint|"
    r"\barxiv\b|\bbiorxiv\b|\bmedrxiv\b|\bresearch square\b|\bspreprints\b|\bosf\b|\brepec\b|"
    r"conference|proceedings|symposium|workshop|book|chapter|thesis|dissertation", re.I)

# COVERAGE CELLS — the empty industry cells the outline promises (task brief), with aliases.
INDUSTRY_CELLS = {
    "healthcare":    [r"health ?care", r"\bhealth\b", r"\bclinical\b", r"\bhospital", r"physician",
                      r"\bnurse", r"\bmedical\b", r"radiolog", r"\bpatient", r"\bdiagnos"],
    "manufacturing": [r"manufactur", r"\bfactor(?:y|ies)\b", r"production line", r"industrial robot",
                      r"\bassembly\b", r"\bshop ?floor"],
    "retail":        [r"\bretail", r"e-?commerce", r"\bcashier", r"\bstore\b", r"\bwarehous", r"\blogistics? worker"],
    "finance":       [r"\bfinanc(?:e|ial)", r"\bbank(?:ing|s)?\b", r"\binsurance\b", r"fintech",
                      r"accounting", r"\baudit", r"\btrading\b", r"wealth management"],
    "education":     [r"\beducation", r"\bteacher", r"\bteaching\b", r"\bstudent", r"classroom",
                      r"\bschool", r"universit", r"higher education", r"\btutor"],
    "agriculture":   [r"agricultur", r"\bfarm(?:ing|er|s)?\b", r"\bcrop", r"\bharvest", r"\blivestock"],
    "transport":     [r"\btransport", r"transportation", r"\blogistics\b", r"\bdriver", r"trucking",
                      r"\bdelivery\b", r"\bmobility\b", r"autonomous vehicle", r"\bwarehouse"],
}
# Cross-industry OUTCOME dimensions (used as cell_served when no industry is named).
OUTCOME_CELLS = {
    "wage_effects":   [r"\bwages?\b", r"\bearnings\b", r"\bsalar", r"wage inequality", r"skill premium"],
    "employment":     [r"\bemployment\b", r"\bunemployment\b", r"\bjobs?\b", r"job (?:loss|displacement|creation)",
                       r"labou?r demand", r"\bhiring\b"],
    "productivity":   [r"productivity", r"\befficiency\b", r"\boutput\b", r"performance"],
    "tasks":          [r"\btasks?\b", r"task (?:automation|displacement)", r"routine"],
    "occupations":    [r"\boccupations?\b", r"\bskills?\b", r"reskilling", r"upskilling", r"skill demand"],
    "org_adoption":   [r"\badoption\b", r"\bfirm", r"\borganiz", r"\bbusiness\b", r"management"],
}

def _compile(patterns): return [re.compile(p, re.I) for p in patterns]
RX_AI_GEN, RX_AI_BROAD, RX_LABOR = _compile(AI_GENERATIVE), _compile(AI_BROAD), _compile(LABOR_CORE)
RX_INDUSTRY = {k: _compile(v) for k, v in INDUSTRY_CELLS.items()}
RX_OUTCOME  = {k: _compile(v) for k, v in OUTCOME_CELLS.items()}

def _hits(rxs, text):
    out = []
    for rx in rxs:
        m = rx.search(text)
        if m: out.append(m.group(0).lower().strip())
    return sorted(set(out))

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# RECORD PARSERS  (metadata + inline abstract only — NO full text fetched)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def _strip(s):  # JATS/HTML abstract -> plain text
    if not s: return ""
    return html.unescape(re.sub(r"<[^>]+>", " ", s)).replace("\n", " ").strip()

def _norm_doi(doi):
    if not doi: return None
    doi = doi.strip().lower()
    doi = re.sub(r"^https?://(dx\.)?doi\.org/", "", doi)
    return doi or None

def parse_crossref(body):
    out = []
    for it in ((body or {}).get("message") or {}).get("items", []) or []:
        di = (it.get("issued") or {}).get("date-parts") or [[None]]
        year = di[0][0] if di and di[0] else None
        out.append({
            "doi": _norm_doi(it.get("DOI")),
            "title": (it.get("title") or [""])[0],
            "venue": (it.get("container-title") or [""])[0] if it.get("container-title") else "",
            "year": year,
            "type": it.get("type"),
            "abstract": _strip(it.get("abstract")),
            "issn": (it.get("ISSN") or []),
            "db": "crossref",
        })
    return out

def _oa_abstract(inv):
    if not inv: return ""
    pos = {}
    for w, idxs in inv.items():
        for i in idxs: pos[i] = w
    return " ".join(pos[i] for i in sorted(pos))[:1200]

def parse_openalex(body):
    out = []
    for it in (body or {}).get("results", []) or []:
        loc = it.get("primary_location") or {}
        src = loc.get("source") or {}
        out.append({
            "doi": _norm_doi(it.get("doi")),
            "title": it.get("title") or it.get("display_name") or "",
            "venue": src.get("display_name") or "",
            "year": it.get("publication_year"),
            "type": it.get("type"),
            "oa_source_type": src.get("type"),       # 'journal' vs 'repository' etc.
            "is_paratext": it.get("is_paratext"),
            "abstract": _oa_abstract(it.get("abstract_inverted_index")),
            "openalex_id": it.get("id"),
            "db": "openalex",
        })
    return out

PARSERS = {"crossref": parse_crossref, "openalex": parse_openalex}

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# ADMISSION GATE
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def is_journal(rec):
    """Journal article only. Working papers / preprints / proceedings / books are NOT journal evidence."""
    if NONJOURNAL_VENUE.search(rec.get("venue") or ""):
        return False, "non-journal venue"
    if rec["db"] == "crossref":
        if rec.get("type") != "journal-article":
            return False, f"crossref type={rec.get('type')}"
        return True, "crossref journal-article"
    if rec["db"] == "openalex":
        if rec.get("is_paratext"):
            return False, "openalex paratext"
        if rec.get("oa_source_type") != "journal":
            return False, f"openalex source.type={rec.get('oa_source_type')}"
        if rec.get("type") not in ("article", None):
            return False, f"openalex type={rec.get('type')}"
        return True, "openalex journal article"
    return False, "unknown db"

def classify(rec):
    text = f"{rec.get('title','')}  {rec.get('abstract','')}"
    ai_gen = _hits(RX_AI_GEN, text)
    ai_broad = _hits(RX_AI_BROAD, text)
    labor = _hits(RX_LABOR, text)
    ai = ai_gen + [a for a in ai_broad if a not in ai_gen]
    on_topic = bool(ai) and bool(labor)
    # cell_served: an industry cell if named (fills an EMPTY coverage cell), else an outcome dimension.
    industries = [c for c, rxs in RX_INDUSTRY.items() if _hits(rxs, text)]
    outcomes = [c for c, rxs in RX_OUTCOME.items() if _hits(rxs, text)]
    if industries:
        cell = industries[0]; cell_kind = "industry"
    elif outcomes:
        cell = outcomes[0]; cell_kind = "cross-industry:outcome"
    else:
        cell = "unassigned"; cell_kind = "outcome"
    return {
        "on_topic": on_topic, "genai_specific": bool(ai_gen),
        "ai_terms": ai, "labor_terms": labor,
        "cell_served": cell, "cell_kind": cell_kind,
        "industries_named": industries, "outcomes_named": outcomes,
    }

def year_ok(y):
    return isinstance(y, int) and 2023 <= y <= 2026

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# THE FRONTIER NEEDS + CUSTOM PER-YEAR BANDS
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
NEEDS = [
    ("genai_employment",  ["generative AI", "employment", "labor market"]),
    ("llm_work_prod",     ["large language models", "work", "productivity", "tasks"]),
    ("chatgpt_jobs",      ["ChatGPT", "jobs", "occupations", "workers"]),
    ("genai_wages_skill", ["generative AI", "wages", "skill demand", "workforce"]),
    ("ai_displacement",   ["artificial intelligence", "job displacement", "automation", "employment"]),
    ("llm_labor_tasks",   ["LLM", "labor market", "occupations", "productivity"]),
]

def year_bands():
    b = []
    for y in (2023, 2024, 2025):
        b.append(R.FrontierBand(f"year_{y}", f"calendar year {y}", ["published"],
                                dt.date(y, 1, 1), dt.date(y, 12, 31)))
    return b

def bands_for(need):
    """recency.py's overlapping frontier bands (the literal spec) PLUS explicit per-year windows."""
    profile_bands = R.resolve_bands(need, PROFILE)      # since_boundary | last_24m | last_12m | ahead_of_print | newly_indexed
    return profile_bands + year_bands()

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# RUN
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PROFILE = R.load_profile()
# rows is DATA — bump the sample depth in memory only (the on-disk profile is untouched).
for dbn in ("crossref", "openalex"):
    PROFILE["databases"][dbn]["rows"] = ROWS

def main():
    acq = Acquirer("recency_frontier", ledger=open_ledger())
    records = {}                      # norm_doi -> merged record
    no_doi = []                       # journal+on-topic records lacking a DOI (kept, keyed by title)
    reach = []                        # per-query reachability rows
    seen_query = set()

    for need_key, terms in NEEDS:
        need = R.RecencyNeed(
            claim_kind="current_adoption_rate", subject_terms=terms, as_of=AS_OF,
            boundary="generative_ai_turn", last_run=R._minus_months(AS_OF, 6),
            unit=f"task72:frontier:{need_key}")
        bands = bands_for(need)
        queries = [q for q in R.build_frontier_queries(need, PROFILE, bands)
                   if q.supported and q.database in ("crossref", "openalex")]
        # ROUTE_PLANNED names every adapter we are about to try (so route completion is meaningful).
        adapters = [f"{q.database}:{q.band}:{q.date_type}" for q in queries]
        acq.plan_route(need.unit, adapters, lane="frontier", claim_kind=need.claim_kind)

        for q in queries:
            if q.url in seen_query:            # identical URL across needs (rare) -> skip duplicate fetch
                continue
            seen_query.add(q.url)
            adapter = f"{q.database}:{q.band}:{q.date_type}"
            resp, body = acq.get_json(need.unit, adapter, q.url)
            total = R._result_count(PROFILE["databases"].get(q.database, {}), body) if resp.ok else None
            parsed = PARSERS[q.database](body) if resp.ok else []
            n_adm = 0
            for rec in parsed:
                if not year_ok(rec.get("year")):
                    continue
                jok, _jr = is_journal(rec)
                if not jok:
                    continue
                cl = classify(rec)
                if not cl["on_topic"]:
                    continue
                rec.update(cl)
                rec["found_via"] = [adapter]
                n_adm += 1
                key = rec["doi"]
                if key:
                    if key in records:
                        _merge(records[key], rec, adapter)
                    else:
                        records[key] = rec
                else:
                    no_doi.append(rec)
            if resp.ok and n_adm:
                acq.candidate(need.unit, adapter, q.url, n_results=n_adm,
                              band=q.band, date_type=q.date_type, sort=q.sort)
            reach.append({
                "need": need_key, "database": q.database, "band": q.band, "date_type": q.date_type,
                "outcome": resp.outcome, "http_status": resp.http_status,
                "total_results_reported": total, "n_admitted": n_adm,
            })
    return finalize(records, no_doi, reach)

def _merge(dst, src, adapter):
    dst.setdefault("found_via", [])
    if adapter not in dst["found_via"]:
        dst["found_via"].append(adapter)
    dst.setdefault("dbs", [dst.get("db")])
    if src.get("db") not in dst["dbs"]:
        dst["dbs"].append(src.get("db"))
    # prefer the richer record's venue/abstract
    if len(src.get("venue") or "") > len(dst.get("venue") or ""):
        dst["venue"] = src["venue"]
    if len(src.get("abstract") or "") > len(dst.get("abstract") or ""):
        dst["abstract"] = src["abstract"]
        dst.update({k: src[k] for k in ("ai_terms", "labor_terms", "genai_specific",
                                        "cell_served", "cell_kind", "industries_named", "outcomes_named")})

def finalize(records, no_doi, reach):
    admitted = list(records.values()) + no_doi
    for r in admitted:
        r.setdefault("dbs", [r.get("db")])
        r["why_relevant"] = (
            ("generative-AI turn: " if r["genai_specific"] else "AI-labor: ")
            + f"AI[{', '.join(r['ai_terms'][:3])}] x WORK[{', '.join(r['labor_terms'][:3])}]"
        )
    admitted.sort(key=lambda r: (-(r.get("year") or 0), not r["genai_specific"], r.get("cell_served","")))

    by_year = Counter(r["year"] for r in admitted)
    by_year_gen = Counter(r["year"] for r in admitted if r["genai_specific"])
    by_cell = Counter(r["cell_served"] for r in admitted)
    by_industry = Counter(i for r in admitted for i in r["industries_named"])
    by_db = Counter(d for r in admitted for d in r["dbs"])

    # reachability ceiling: max reported total per (database, band) across needs (queries overlap heavily)
    ceil = defaultdict(int)
    throttled = [x for x in reach if x["outcome"] != "RESPONDED"]
    for x in reach:
        if x["total_results_reported"]:
            ceil[(x["database"], x["band"])] = max(ceil[(x["database"], x["band"])], x["total_results_reported"])

    out = {
        "generated": dt.datetime.now().isoformat(timespec="seconds"),
        "as_of": AS_OF.isoformat(), "boundary": "generative_ai_turn (2023-01-01)",
        "method": "recency.py frontier lane (date-windowed, date-sorted, citation-sort refused) via "
                  "acquisition.Acquirer; Crossref + OpenAlex; metadata + inline abstract only, NO full text",
        "admission_rule": "journal-article AND (AI term) AND (CORE labor term), word boundaries; "
                          "working papers/preprints/proceedings excluded; year in 2023-2026",
        "counts": {
            "admitted_total": len(admitted),
            "with_doi": len(records), "without_doi": len(no_doi),
            "by_year": dict(sorted(by_year.items())),
            "by_year_generative_ai_specific": dict(sorted(by_year_gen.items())),
            "generative_ai_specific_total": sum(1 for r in admitted if r["genai_specific"]),
            "by_cell_served": dict(by_cell.most_common()),
            "by_industry_cell_touched": dict(by_industry.most_common()),
            "by_database": dict(by_db),
        },
        "reachability_ceiling_by_db_band": {f"{k[0]}:{k[1]}": v for k, v in sorted(ceil.items())},
        "n_queries": len(reach),
        "n_queries_not_responded": len(throttled),
        "not_responded_detail": throttled,
        "records": [
            {k: r.get(k) for k in ("doi", "title", "venue", "year", "why_relevant", "cell_served",
                                   "cell_kind", "genai_specific", "ai_terms", "labor_terms",
                                   "industries_named", "dbs", "found_via", "abstract")}
            for r in admitted
        ],
    }
    (HERE / "frontier_2023_2025.json").write_text(json.dumps(out, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False))
    # compact records-only file for downstream binding (doi/title/venue/year/why/cell)
    (HERE / "frontier_2023_2025_records.json").write_text(json.dumps(out["records"], indent=2, ensure_ascii=False))
    print(json.dumps({k: out[k] for k in ("counts", "reachability_ceiling_by_db_band", "n_queries",
                                          "n_queries_not_responded")}, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False))
    return out

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Union the frontier records from both date-sorted sweeps (run1 + run2), dedup, recount.

Both sweeps ran recency.py's frontier lane via acquisition.Acquirer; because OpenAlex throttled us under
concurrent shared-IP load, neither single sweep saw everything. THE LAW: a 429 is THROTTLED, not "no
evidence" — so the honest reachable set is the UNION of what the sweeps that DID respond returned. Dedup
by normalized DOI, then by normalized title for the DOI-less residue.
"""
import json, re, sys, datetime as dt
from pathlib import Path
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

HERE = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
FILES = [HERE / "run1_records.json", HERE / "run2_records.json"]

def norm_title(t): return re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", " ", (t or "").lower()).strip()

def merge():
    by_doi, by_title = {}, {}
    n_in = 0
    for f in FILES:
        if not f.exists():
            print(f"[skip missing] {f.name}", file=sys.stderr); continue
        recs = json.loads(f.read_text())
        n_in += len(recs)
        for r in recs:
            doi = r.get("doi")
            if doi:
                cur = by_doi.get(doi)
                if not cur:
                    by_doi[doi] = r
                else:
                    for a in (r.get("found_via") or []):
                        if a not in cur.setdefault("found_via", []): cur["found_via"].append(a)
                    for dbn in (r.get("dbs") or []):
                        if dbn not in cur.setdefault("dbs", []): cur["dbs"].append(dbn)
                    if len(r.get("abstract") or "") > len(cur.get("abstract") or ""):
                        cur["abstract"] = r["abstract"]
                    if len(r.get("venue") or "") > len(cur.get("venue") or ""):
                        cur["venue"] = r["venue"]
            else:
                nt = norm_title(r.get("title"))
                if nt: by_title.setdefault(nt, r)
    doi_titles = {norm_title(r.get("title")) for r in by_doi.values()}
    no_doi = [r for nt, r in by_title.items() if nt not in doi_titles]
    admitted = list(by_doi.values()) + no_doi
    admitted.sort(key=lambda r: (-(r.get("year") or 0), not r.get("genai_specific"), r.get("cell_served","")))

    by_year = Counter(r["year"] for r in admitted)
    by_year_gen = Counter(r["year"] for r in admitted if r.get("genai_specific"))
    by_cell = Counter(r["cell_served"] for r in admitted)
    by_industry = Counter(i for r in admitted for i in (r.get("industries_named") or []))
    by_db = Counter(d for r in admitted for d in (r.get("dbs") or []))
    y2325 = sum(v for k, v in by_year.items() if k in (2023, 2024, 2025))

    out = {
        "generated": dt.datetime.now().isoformat(timespec="seconds"),
        "method": "UNION of two recency.py frontier-lane sweeps (date-windowed, date-sorted, citation-sort "
                  "refused) via acquisition.Acquirer; Crossref + OpenAlex; metadata + inline abstract only, "
                  "NO full text. Union is honest because OpenAlex throttled (429) under shared-IP load and a "
                  "429 is THROTTLED, never 'no evidence'.",
        "admission_rule": "journal-article AND (AI term) AND (CORE labor term), word boundaries; working "
                          "papers/preprints/proceedings excluded; year 2023-2026",
        "counts": {
            "admitted_total": len(admitted),
            "records_in_before_dedup": n_in,
            "in_2023_2025": y2325,

Everything checks out: 299 records, 298 unique DOIs, zero duplicate DOIs, all in-window, all with the required fields. No forbidden files touched (I only wrote under `outputs/acquisition_campaign/` and appended to the shared ledger via the Acquirer; `recency.py` and the profile were used unmodified — `rows` was bumped in memory only).

---

## MODALITY 3 — 2023-2025 FRONTIER: how much journal literature is actually reachable

**Method (faithful to the frontier lane + THE LAW).** Ran `recency.py`'s FRONTIER LANE unmodified: 6 targeted generative-AI×labor query needs (generative AI / LLM / ChatGPT × employment, wages, jobs, occupations, tasks, productivity, skill demand), each expanded into overlapping date-windowed, **date-sorted (never citation-sorted)** bands — `since_boundary` (2023-01-01), `last_12_months`, `online-ahead-of-print`, plus explicit per-calendar-year windows (2023/2024/2025) so a desc-sort sweep doesn't bunch at 2026 — against **Crossref DATE filters + OpenAlex**. Every fetch went through `acquisition.Acquirer` (THE ONE DOOR). **No full text fetched** — metadata + inline abstract only. Admission gate: `journal-article AND (AI term) AND (CORE labor term)`, **word-boundary** regexes (ResNet/skin-cancer/crop-yield papers correctly rejected — AI but no labor anchor); working papers / preprints / proceedings excluded (discovery leads, not journal evidence).

**Headline reachability: abundant, and we are sampling-limited, not supply-limited.**
- **299 deduped on-topic journal articles** admitted (298 unique DOIs): **206 in 2023-2025**, plus **93 in 2026-YTD**.
- Counts by year: **2023=55, 2024=58, 2025=93, 2026=93**.
- **Generative-AI-specific slice: 60** (2023=14, 2024=4, 2025=20, 2026=22). The 2024 dip is a sampling/throttle artifact, not a real trend.
- These 299 came from only `rows=100`/query off the date-sorted top. The raw date-windowed relevance funnels are enormous — **OpenAlex ~23K–31K hits/yr, Crossref ~260K–325K hits/yr** before the on-topic gate — so on-topic 2023-2025 journal literature on AI & work is plentiful and trivially reachable by date; recall here is capped by sampling depth, not scarcity.

**The seven empty coverage cells now have candidate leads** (heuristic keyword tag on title+abstract): education 47, healthcare 39, finance 21, manufacturing 18, transport 12, retail 6, agriculture 2. **Caveat:** `cell_served` is a first-pass tag and fires on incidental mentions — a Columbia tax-law paper got tagged "agriculture," a macro OECD paper "transport." Industry counts are leads to verify, not confirmed domain evidence; agriculture (n=2) is unreliable. healthcare/finance/manufacturing are the more genuine.

**Venue-quality caveat (matters for THE LAW).** The on-topic COUNT is high, but venue quality is heterogeneous. A credible peer-reviewed core exists — *Journal of Applied Psychology*, *Regional Studies*, *Journal of Computer-Mediated Communication*, *Journal of the Association for Information Systems*, "Economics of ChatGPT: a labor-market view on the occupational impact" — but the majority are regional/low-impact venues, and some are dubious (a *Journal of Advanced Zoology* piece on "Impact of ChatGPT on Industry"). Only ~30-40 sit in recognizable mid/high-tier journals. **A venue-authority filter (DOAJ/Scopus/tier gate) is the required next step before any of these enter the corpus.**

**Throttle honesty (THE LAW upheld).** OpenAlex 429'd repeatedly under concurrent shared-IP load from the other live wheels. Those queries are recorded as **THROTTLED**, never as "no evidence" — so the counts above are a **lower bound**. Run-1: 9/90 queries THROTTLED (recorded as such). A 3s-spacing re-run hit a worse 429 storm and was stopped as low-ROI; the reported set is the honest union of what responded.

**Deliverables (all under the allowed dir):**
- `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/acquisition_campaign/frontier_2023_2025_FINAL.json` — canonical result: 299 records with `doi/title/venue/year/why_relevant/cell_served` (+ `genai_specific`, matched ai/labor terms, `dbs`, `found_via`, abstract) and all aggregate counts by year/cell/db.
- `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/acquisition_campaign/frontier_harvest.py` — harvester (recency.py frontier lane via Acquirer).
- `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/acquisition_campaign/run1_frontier.json` — full run with per-query **reachability ceilings** + THROTTLED detail.
- `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/acquisition_campaign/merge_runs.py` — union/dedup.

**Recommended next step:** run this record set through a venue-authority/quality gate, then `provenance.py` binding (manifestation_id + content_hash) for the surviving ~30-60 credible journal articles — that is the real 2023-2025 evidence the corpus is missing.
